recipes.title: Authentic Thai green curry (chicken)

ingredients.rawText: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken thigh, 1-inch pieces
ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thigh, 1-inch pieces

ingredients.rawText: 3.5 tbsp green curry paste (Maeploy or Aroy-D recommended)
ingredients.name: green curry paste (Maeploy or Aroy-D recommended)

ingredients.rawText: 0.5 lb Thai eggplant (or 19 oz canned bamboo shoots, drained)
ingredients.name: Thai eggplant (or 19 oz canned bamboo shoots, drained)

steps: Optional: pound Thai basil for paste (15 leaves, julienned), fingerroot (1 piece), fermented shrimp paste (1 tsp), and green curry paste (3½ tbsp) in a mortar (or blend with chicken stock (little) until smooth).
Bring coconut milk (~¾ cup of 1¾ cups total) to a full boil; add green curry paste (plus mortar blend if using); cook until coconut oil separates ((3–5 min)), or proceed if paste is thick.

Add boneless chicken thigh (1 lb); stir into paste. Pour in remaining coconut milk, chicken stock (1 cup), palm sugar (2 tbsp), and about half the fish sauce ((~¾ tbsp) of (1½–2 tbsp) total; adjust later).
Bruise makrut lime leaves (5); tear into chunks (discard tough stems). Simmer gently (10–15 min) until chicken is fork-tender.

Add Thai eggplant (½ lb) (or bamboo shoots (drained)) and cook until just tender; turn off heat. Stir in red bell pepper (¼, julienned) and Thai basil leaves (1 cup). Taste; add remaining fish sauce as needed. Serve with jasmine rice.
recipes.notes:
Paste strength varies by brand—taste and adjust. Source: Hot Thai Kitchen — https://hot-thai-kitchen.com/green-curry-new-2/
Chicken breast: marinate in 1 tbsp fish sauce; add later per site instructions.

recipes.title: Kimchi stew (kimchi-jjigae)

ingredients.rawText: 0.5 lb pork shoulder or pork belly, sliced
ingredients.name: pork shoulder or pork belly, sliced

steps: Anchovy stock: Simmer dried anchovies (7, cleaned), radish (⅓ cup sliced), green onion roots (from 3 onions), and dried kelp (~4×5 in) in water (4 cups) (20 min) medium-high; (5 min) low; strain (~2 cups for stew).
In a shallow pot, add kimchi (1 lb), kimchi brine (¼ cup), pork (½ lb), and onion (1, sliced). Slice green onions (2) diagonally and add.
Add kosher salt (1 tsp), sugar (2 tsp), gochugaru (2 tsp), gochujang (1 tbsp), and sesame oil (1 tsp). Pour in anchovy stock (2 cups). Cover; cook (10 min) medium-high.
Stir seasonings; lay tofu (½ package, optional) on top. Cover; cook (10–15 min) medium.
Top with chopped green onion (1); serve immediately with steamed rice.
recipes.notes:
Serves 2 with side dishes, 4 without. Source: Maangchi — https://www.maangchi.com/recipe/kimchi-jjigae
Kimchi stew is thicker and saltier than kimchi soup (guk).

recipes.title: Korean seaweed soup (miyeok guk)

ingredients.rawText: 120 g beef chuck or round steak, thin slices
ingredients.name: beef chuck or round steak, thin slices

ingredients.rawText: Steamed rice and Korean side dishes to serve
ingredients.name: Steamed rice and Korean side dishes to serve

steps: Soak dried seaweed (15 g) in cold water (5–10 min) until expanded. Rinse (2–3 times); drain and squeeze dry. Cut to little-finger lengths if needed.
Toss beef (120 g) with fine sea salt (¼ tsp) and black pepper; set aside.
Heat a medium pot over medium. Add sesame oil (1 tbsp), seaweed, and beef; stir until beef is partly cooked (~2 min).
Add soy sauce (1½ tbsp), minced garlic (1 tsp), and water (5 cups). Cover; boil medium to medium-high until beef is fully cooked (10–15 min). Adjust salt if needed.
Serve warm with steamed rice and side dishes.
recipes.notes: 3–4 servings. Often served on birthdays in Korea. Source: My Korean Kitchen — https://mykoreankitchen.com/korean-seaweed-soup-miyeok-guk/

recipes.title: Lemon garlic salmon tray bake

ingredients.rawText: 4 piece salmon fillets (~180g each, skin on or off)
ingredients.name: salmon fillets (~180g each, skin on or off)

ingredients.rawText: 2 tsp extra virgin olive oil (for veg)
ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil (for veg)

steps: Mix lemon zest (1 tsp), lemon juice (1 tbsp), extra virgin olive oil (2 tbsp), Dijon mustard (1 tsp), garlic (2 cloves, grated), kosher salt (½ tsp), and black pepper (¼ tsp). Slather over tops and sides of salmon fillets (4× ~180 g). Marinate (1 hour) if time allows.
Preheat oven grill/broiler as hot as possible (~280°C / 525°F). Rack (~20 cm) from heat.

Toss asparagus (3 bunches) and cherry or grape tomatoes (200 g) with olive oil for veg (2 tsp) and salt & pepper for veg (¼ tsp). Spread on a large tray; nestle salmon fillets (4) with space between; spray with olive oil spray.
Grill/broil (~11 min) until salmon flakes; aim internal temperature (~50°C) for medium-rare (see notes).
Plate salmon and vegetables; grate Parmesan over vegetables; add lemon wedges and parsley if using; serve.
recipes.notes:
Marinade optional but improves flavour. Source: RecipeTin Eats — https://www.recipetineats.com/lemon-garlic-salmon-tray-bake-easy-healthy/
Doneness: ~50°C pull for juicy salmon; ~60°C for more done.

recipes.title: No-knead sourdough bread

ingredients.rawText: 200 g freshly ground whole wheat flour
ingredients.name: freshly ground whole wheat flour

ingredients.rawText: 250 g bread flour (can use more AP instead)
ingredients.name: bread flour (can use more AP instead)

steps: Feed active sourdough starter (200 g) (4–12 h) ahead so it is lively and bubbly.
Combine warm water (650 g) with unbleached AP flour (500 g), whole wheat flour (200 g), and bread flour (250 g); rest (30 min) (autolyse).
Add starter (200 g); dimple in with a wet hand. Sprinkle salt (20 g); mix by hand (~5 min); rest (30 min) covered.
Stretch-and-fold: first (3) rounds every (15 min); next (3) rounds every (30 min).
Bulk ferment until doubled (time varies). Split gently into two loaves; shape; bench rest (15–20 min) uncovered; shape again.
Proof seam-up in a floured banneton or towel-lined bowl; refrigerate covered (12–15 h).
Preheat Dutch oven to 260°C (500°F) (~1 h). Score cold dough; bake covered (~20 min) at 260°C (500°F); uncover, reduce to 245°C (475°F), bake (~25 min) more until deep brown.
recipes.notes:
Long timeline (mix day one, bake next). Source: Farmhouse on Boone — https://www.farmhouseonboone.com/no-knead-sourdough-bread/
Use a kitchen scale for best results; starter must be active.

recipes.title: Pork roast with crispy crackling

ingredients.rawText: 3 kg boneless skin-on pork shoulder, unrolled, not scored
ingredients.name: boneless skin-on pork shoulder, unrolled, not scored

ingredients.rawText: 2 cup dry white wine (or cider; see notes)
ingredients.name: dry white wine (or cider; see notes)

steps: Pat pork skin dry (overnight uncovered in fridge ideal if vac-packed). Preheat oven to 220°C / 430°F (200°C fan).
Season flesh with salt (1½ tsp of 3 tsp total), black pepper (1¼ tsp), fennel seeds (2 tsp), and olive oil (1 tbsp); rub into crevices.
Flip skin-side up; rub skin with olive oil (1 tsp) and remaining salt (1½ tsp) evenly. Place halved garlic (1 bulb) and onions (2) in roasting pan; set pork skin-up on top.
Pour white wine (2 cups) into pan without wetting skin. Roast uncovered at 160°C / 320°F (140°C fan) for 2½ hours.
After 1½ hours, level warped skin with foil balls; return for remaining 1 hour. Add water to pan if drying; re-salt any bald skin patches.
Increase oven to 250°C / 485°F (or max). Roast (30 min), rotating pan, until crackling is crisp all over; foil-patch done areas if needed.
Rest pork loosely tented (20 min). Slice with a serrated knife; serve gravy beside crackling (not on top).

Gravy: Skim 3 tbsp fat from pan into a saucepan. Strain pan juices (press onion/garlic); skim excess fat. Cook flour (¼ cup) in fat (1 min); whisk in chicken stock (2 cups) and strained juices; simmer until syrupy (~3 min); season.
recipes.notes:
10–12 servings. Boneless flat shoulder gives best crackle. Source: RecipeTin Eats — https://www.recipetineats.com/pork-shoulder-roast-with-crispy-crackling/
Fridge-dry skin overnight if supermarket vac-packed. Separate crackling from meat when storing leftovers.

recipes.title: Simple feta salad

ingredients.rawText: Extra veg as you like (cucumber, capsicum, red onion, avocado, etc.)
ingredients.name: Extra veg as you like (cucumber, capsicum, red onion, avocado, etc.)

steps: Wash and dry mixed loose salad leaves; add to a large bowl.
Add tomatoes (2, halved or wedged), crumbled feta (150 g), and any extra veg you like (cucumber, capsicum, red onion, avocado, etc.).
Whisk extra virgin olive oil (3 tbsp), balsamic vinegar (1 tbsp), Dijon mustard (1 tsp, optional), and honey or sugar (½ tsp, optional); season with salt and black pepper.
Toss salad lightly with dressing just before serving.
recipes.notes: Flexible house salad — swap in whatever crisp veg you have on hand.

recipes.title: Spaghetti Bolognese

ingredients.rawText: 500 g beef mince (or half pork/half beef)
ingredients.name: beef mince (or half pork/half beef)

steps: Heat olive oil (1½ tbsp) in a large pot over medium-high. Add onion (1, finely chopped) and garlic (2 cloves, minced); cook (~3 min) until golden.
Raise heat; add beef mince (500 g) and brown, breaking it up as it cooks.
Pour in dry red wine (½ cup); simmer (1 min), scraping the pan.

Add crushed tomatoes (800 g), tomato paste (2 tbsp), beef bouillon cubes (2, crumbled), white sugar (2 tsp, if needed), Worcestershire sauce (2 tsp), bay leaves (2), fresh thyme (2 sprigs), salt (¾ tsp), and black pepper (½ tsp). Simmer (20–30 min) uncovered (add water if too thick). Optional: cover and simmer on low (2–2.5 h) with extra water for deeper flavour.
Cook dried spaghetti (400 g) one minute shy of package time; reserve pasta water. Optionally toss pasta in the sauce with reserved water (~½ cup) until glossy. Serve with Parmesan and parsley.
recipes.notes:
Source: RecipeTin Eats — https://www.recipetineats.com/spaghetti-bolognese/
Slow-simmer optional; better next day.
